notifications.title: Central Communications Christmas and New Years hours 2024/2025
notifications.body:
The company will operate under "brown out" status from December 20, 2024 through January 6, 2025. During this period there will be restrictions on number porting, reduced support capacity, and all project work will be frozen.

A "black out" period runs December 23, 2024 to January 3, 2025 during which only emergency responses will be handled.

If you require holiday greeting messages for your phone system, please request these now.

Normal operations resume January 6, 2025.
notifications.author: Paul Willard

notifications.title: iPhone users and NX Witness
notifications.body: Some iPhone users may be experiencing problems with NX Witness. This issue has been escalated to NXVMS for investigation.
notifications.author: Paul Willard

notifications.title: NX Witness Cloud account issues
notifications.body: Cloud account users are facing login problems where they authenticate successfully but experience an immediate logout. This issue has been escalated to NXVMS for investigation.
notifications.author: Paul Willard

notifications.title: Nx Witness Cloud Maintenance
notifications.body: Nx Witness Cloud access is temporarily unavailable due to maintenance updates. Camera servers continue operating normally and recording functions remain intact. Customers cannot access their camera systems through the cloud interface during this period. This type of maintenance does not normally take very long and service will resume when the cloud platform returns online.
notifications.author: Paul Willard

notifications.title: CCL Christmas hours 2023/2024
notifications.body:
Central Communications will operate under reduced capacity from December 20, 2023 through January 8, 2024. During this period there will be no phone number porting, reduced support availability, and no system modifications.

From December 23, 2023 to January 3, 2024, only emergency work will be undertaken. A single phone failure is not an emergency if alternatives exist, nor are offline cameras unless legally required for surveillance.

Please contact us via the main office line rather than personal mobile numbers during this period.
notifications.author: Paul Willard

notifications.title: Friday 13th October phone line outages
notifications.body: We are aware of an upstream phone provider having issues. All phones and phone lines will likely be offline this morning (Friday 13th October).
notifications.author: Paul Willard

notifications.title: Nationwide internet issue
notifications.body: An upstream internet provider is experiencing a malfunction causing some customers to be offline. Engineering teams are actively investigating the issue.
notifications.author: Paul Willard

notifications.title: 2Degrees connectivity issues
notifications.body: Incoming calls from 2Degrees mobile phones to our office are currently being blocked. As an alternative, please call from another mobile carrier or a landline while we investigate.
notifications.author: Paul Willard

notifications.title: Phone issues with Wanna Internet / Purelink
notifications.body: Customers using Wanna Internet / Purelink as their provider are experiencing intermittent phone connection problems. Purelink have indicated they are experiencing widespread issues and do not have an immediate solution. If you would like to change providers, we can facilitate this quickly — please get in touch.
notifications.author: Paul Willard

notifications.title: Taumarunui Central outage (Internet)
notifications.body: Customers in Taumarunui Central may be experiencing a loss of Internet service. The outage started 18th July 2022 at 01:26. Techs are working on it.
notifications.author: Paul Willard

notifications.title: Spark Mobile - Matamata area
notifications.body: We have identified an ongoing issue with Spark Mobile phones being unable to make calls in the Matamata area. We believe the problem relates to VoLTE technology and have lodged a fault with Spark Corporate services.
notifications.author: Paul Willard
